title: toxicodendrum cobbe
intro: Toxicodendrum cobbe, also known as the lacquer tree or poison tree, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family, which is native to Australia and Asia. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic resin that causes skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. However, in traditional medicine, the resin is used to treat various ailments, including inflammation, pain, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the lacquer tree's resin is often extracted and used to create topical creams, ointments, and lacquers, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
